grading_notes: Each assertion is graded pass/partial/fail across both attempts. Skill is judged on whether it gave Claude the material to consistently produce the right answer.

assertions.claim: Mentions Plan struct in plannodes.h with `Plan plan;` first field
Mentions PlanState struct in execnodes.h, embedding PlanState (not ScanState because not a scan)
Lists the five core executor functions (ExecInit/Exec/ExecEnd/ExecReScan) and notes ExecProcNode is set inside ExecInit
Wires into all THREE dispatch switches: ExecInitNode + ExecEndNode in execProcnode.c, ExecReScan in execAmi.c
Mentions meson.build + Makefile + header include in execProcnode.c alphabetical block
Mentions EXPLAIN integration in commands/explain.c
Mentions set_plan_references / setrefs.c if the node carries expressions
Calls out the no-malloc / use-palloc memory rule and the close-relations-in-ExecEnd rule
assertions.attempt_B: partial - mentioned palloc but did not enumerate ExecEnd relation-closing rule as separate pitfall in B

assertions.claim: Distinguishes Path (planner currency, cost-comparable) from Plan (executor instruction)
Identifies create_plan as the entry point with create_plan_recurse switching on best_path->pathtype
Cites correct file:line (createplan.c:339 / 390)
Places set_plan_references AFTER create_plan returns
Explains what set_plan_references actually does: Var renumbering to INNER_VAR/OUTER_VAR/scan attno
Notes finalrtable assembly / relationOids collection / plan-cache invalidation items
assertions.attempt_A: partial - mentions finalrtable and relationOids but not invalItems explicitly

assertions.claim: Warns new Plan nodes carrying expressions must be added to fix_*_references_walker

evals.prompt_summary: Unrecognized node type after adding planner-side scaffolding

assertions.claim: Identifies ExecReScan lives in execAmi.c, not execProcnode.c — the likely miss
Distinguishes Plan-tag dispatch (ExecInitNode) vs PlanState-tag dispatch (ExecEndNode, ExecReScan)
assertions.attempt_B: partial - mentioned but less explicit in B

assertions.claim: Cites file:line for the three switches
Reminds to include the new header in execProcnode.c alphabetical block
Suggests checking gen_node_support.pl generated NodeTags for both Plan and PlanState types
Asks WHICH switch is firing (init/end/rescan) to localize the bug
assertions.attempt_A: partial - implies it via the early/cleanup/rescan walkthrough

assertions.claim: Considers MultiExecProcNode but correctly notes it does not apply to a scan-returning-tuples

overall.headline: Skill is operational. All three answers were correct and grounded in the right file:line cites. The two attempts were highly consistent — variance is low, which is the iteration goal.
gaps_observed: MultiExecProcNode was nearly forgotten in eval-3 attempt A — it's listed in SKILL.md A.2 but easy to skip when not obviously relevant.
The skill could surface the Plan-tag-vs-PlanState-tag asymmetry more loudly. Both attempts caught it but it deserves explicit billing as a common pitfall.
`fix_*_references_walker` family is mentioned only in the Common Mistakes section; it would help to include a one-line cite path.
